STIPULATION AND ORDER FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS DENIED AS MOOT. BOTH THE OPPOSITION PLEADING AND DEFENDANT'S REPLY PLEADING REFLECT THAT RESPONSES TO THE DISCOVERY WHICH IS THE SUBJECT OF THE MOTION WERE SERVED WITHOUT OBJECTION ON JUNE 19, 2019. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE SUM OF $360.00 IS AWARDED AS AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO BE PAID WITHIN TWENTY DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. NOTED IS BOTH PARTIES FAILED TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.7 IN THAT COURTESY COPIES OF MOTION PLEADINGS WERE NOT LODGED WITH THE DEPARTMENT 301, WHICH RULE SHOULD BE FOLLOWED FOR FUTURE FILINGS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM STEVEN STEIN, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Answer To Defendants Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Attorney J.P. Schreiber of Ogletree, Deakins 415-442-4810 appeared for defendant. No other appearances.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion to compel is denied as moot. Both the opposition pleading and defendant's reply pleading reflect that responses to the discovery which is the subject of the motion were served without objection on June 19, 2019. Monetary sanctions in the sum of $360.00 is awarded as against plaintiff's counsel to be paid within twenty days of the date of this order. Noted is both parties failed to comply with Local Rule 2.7 in that courtesy copies of motion pleadings were not lodged with the Department 301, which Rule should be followed for future filings. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)
